title: Data insertion exception in version 8.0.27
description:
We found a serious problem. When inserting with insert select statement, there are data exceptions。
How to repeat:
CREATE TABLE test(ID INT,FIELDNAME VARCHAR(100));
INSERT INTO test VALUES(1,'36245');

The result of the query is displayed：

SELECT 
  MAX(id)+1 ,
  '27141' id2
FROM
  test a 
  GROUP BY id2 ;

----------------------------------------
MAX(id)+1  id2     
---------  --------
        2  27141   

However, if you insert this result into the test table：
INSERT INTO test 
SELECT 
  MAX(id)+1 ,
  '27141' id2
FROM
  test a 
  GROUP BY id2 ;

select * from test;

    ID  FIELDNAME  
------  -----------
     1  36245      
(NULL)  27141      

The ID of the data inserted above turned out to be null
Suggested fix:
We changed the way we wrote the sentence, but it was correct：

INSERT INTO test 
SELECT 
  MAX(id+1),
  '27141' id2
FROM
  test a 
  GROUP BY id2
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test2(ID INT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY, FIELDNAME VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL, CREATED_AT TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test2 (ID, FIELDNAME) SELECT MAX(ID)+1, 'Test3' FROM test2;
SELECT ID, FIELDNAME FROM test2 WHERE ID IS NULL;
key_bug_features: Inserting with MAX(id)+1 causes null ID values
